name: Huan Jie (Boxu)
page_title: Huan Jie (Boxu) 桓階 (伯緒) [Han, Zhang Xian, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	We consider Huan Jie (桓階) of novel chapters 7 and 8 to be the same person as Huan Jie (桓楷) of chapters 79 and 80. Historic records have Huan Jie serving both forces with no reference to separate people. [Rafe: GoS; SGZ: Wei 22]

Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	We consider Huan Jie (桓階) of novel chapters 7 and 8 to be the same person as Huan Jie (桓楷) of chapters 79 and 80. Historic records have Huan Jie serving both forces with no reference to separate people. [Rafe: GoS; SGZ: Wei 22]

	Linxiang county, Changsha commandery

	 Other Names 
	Huan Ji (桓楷), Huan Kai

	 Name Notes 
	Huan Jie (桓階) in novel chapters 7 and 8; Huan Ji in the threekingdoms.com edition chapters 7 and 8 and Huan Jie (桓楷) in chapters 79 and 80. We recognize them as the same person; see ‘Fact vs. Fiction’ for more information. Huan K’ai (Huan Kai) in the C.H. Brewitt-Taylor translation.

sections.biography-sanguo-yanyi:
Huan Jie was styled Boxu. Both his grandfather and father served in administrative positions.

Around the year 190, he was recommended as Filial and Incorrupt by the Grand Administrator of Changsha, Sun Jian, and he later served in the central government as a Gentleman of the Secretariat. He left the government to go back to his hometown when his father died. When Sun Jian died in battle against Liu Biao, Huan Jie went to Liu Biao with the request to bury Sun Jian. Liu Biao was impressed by his sense of honor and allowed him to bury Sun Jian.

In 200, Liu Biao sided with Yuan Shao against Cao Cao. Huan Jie was dissatisfied with his choice, so he urged the Grand Administrator of Changsha to rebel against Liu Biao. Huan Jie said, “Regarding those who rebelled but who are not based off righteousness, there are none that have not been defeated. Ergo, the Influential Duke of Qi led the various lords to esteem the Zhou; the Civil Duke of Jin expelled Shudai to receive the King Xiang of Zhou. Presently, the Yuan clan has rebelled, and Governor Liu responds with him. He is taking the road of disaster. Sir, you must want to establish your merits with enlightenment and righteousness, for that will preserve your fortune and distance yourself from disaster. You should not be doing the same thing as him!” Zhang Ci replied, “If so, what to do, and is it possible?” Huan Jie explained, saying, “Although Excellency Cao is weak, he upholds righteousness and has also risen up, saving the imperial government from danger. He, receiving the royal mandate, suppresses the guilty. Who would dare not obey? Now if you incite the four commanderies to protect the Three Rivers to wait for his arrival, then you can be his inner agent! Is this not also possible?” Zhang Ci assented, saying, “I agree.” Zhang Ci thus rebelled against Liu Biao along with three bordering commanderies, and he sent envoys to Cao Cao. Although Cao Cao was too occupied with Yuan Shao to send help, he admired Huan Jie for laying out the plans for the rebellion. Liu Biao took swift action, crushing the rebellion. Huan Jie went into hiding thereafter. After some time, Liu Biao attempted to recruit him to be Libationer Clerk and wanted to give Lady Cai, the younger sister to his wife, in marriage. Huan Jie explained he was already married and he refused all attempts to accept the post. He also claimed illness to avoid further contact.

When Cao Cao took over Jing province in 208, he recruited Huan Jie to be a Registrar to the Assisting Chancellor, then promoted him to Grand Administrator of Zhao Commandery.

When the State of Wei was established for Cao Cao, Huan Jie served as Household General of the Rapid Tigers and Palace Attendant in it. At the time, Cao Cao had not decided on his heir, but he favored the younger Cao Zhi over the older Cao Pi. Huan Jie earnestly explained several times, whether it be through open advice or secret messages, that Cao Pi had good virtuous traits and was older, thus he should naturally be the heir. One of the protests of Huan Jie went like this, “At present, the position of Crown Prince is ranked superior to the other sons. His renown would be known within the Seas, he would have benevolence, he would have wisdom, he would be broad-minded, and would have integrity. There should be none in the world that have not heard of such qualities. Yet, Your Majesty has just asked me about Cao Zhi. I am, sincerely, astonished by this.”

Mao Jie and Xu Yi were renowned for their honesty, and they were disliked by Ding Yi for refusing to associate with him. Ding Yi spoke repeatedly of their faults, and it was only because of the help of Huan Jie that they maintained their posts.

He was eventually promoted to Secretariat and Director of Nominations.

NOVEL

Huan Jie, styled Boxu, originally served in Wu, where he was sent to Liu Biao to negotiate the return of Sun Jian’s body in exchange for their prisoner, Huang Zu. He later found himself in service to Wei, where he was heavily involved with Emperor Xian’s abdication in favor of Cao Pi, ending the Han Dynasty.

full_text:
Huan Jie (Boxu) 桓階 (伯緒)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Huan Jie (桓階), styledBoxu (伯緒), was born in Linxiang county, Changsha commandery. Negotiated the return of Sun Jian’s body. Involved with Xian’s abdication. Huan Jie was affiliated with the Han Dynasty, Zhang Xian, and the Wei Kingdom. Huan Jie lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySanguo Yanyi
